Content_Sketch: The first thing that disappears is not a building. It is the internet. No one asked your opinion about the war. It just arrived.
Illustration_Note: Dark screen with VPN spinner icon failing - text overlay on black

Content_Sketch: Glass of water on kitchen counter. It shakes. You feel the vibration in your feet before you hear the sound.
Illustration_Note: POV: stickman hands gripping kitchen counter edge - glass of water rippling

Content_Sketch: You start counting. You do not remember when you started.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT standing in dark apartment - phone screen glow on face - no signal icon

Content_Sketch: You are a software engineer in Tehran. You build payment apps for a company that cannot process international transactions. You are good at your job. Your job exists inside sanctions.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT at desk with laptop in modern office - Tehran skyline through window - traffic on Hemmat visible

Content_Sketch: Tehran is 9 million people complaining about traffic. University students smoke outside bookshops. You argue about Persepolis on the metro. The barista misspells your name. Saffron ice cream on Valiasr every Friday.
Illustration_Note: Split vignettes: metro crowd - cafe with misspelled cup - ice cream shop on tree-lined street - stickman couple walking

Content_Sketch: Daughter draws a cat on paper. Tapes it to the fridge. The regime is background noise - oppressive but familiar. You have learned where the lines are. You live inside them.
Illustration_Note: Home interior: DAUGHTER drawing at table - cat picture on fridge - warm lighting - normal domestic scene

Content_Sketch: Sanctions already there. Insulin costs 3 months salary. Pharmacy shelves have gaps. You thought these things would always be there.
Illustration_Note: Pharmacy interior - stickman SUBJECT looking at shelf with empty slots - price tag visible - shift to ice cream shop and metro as memory flash

Content_Sketch: Internet dies in layers. Instagram Monday. WhatsApp Tuesday. VPN spins Wednesday and never connects. Mother calls from neighbor's landline. There is no news. That is the news.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT staring at phone - VPN icon spinning - MOTHER on old landline through split panel - dark apartment

Content_Sketch: First airstrike hits 12 kilometers away. Vibration in your feet before the sound. Glass of water shakes on counter. Daughter screams. Power cuts mid-grab. In the dark your neighbor prays through the wall.
Illustration_Note: Apartment shaking - glass of water vibrating on counter - DAUGHTER crying - WIFE grabbing her - lights cutting to black

Content_Sketch: You count. One. Two. Three. If the next one is closer you move. If not you stay. You do not pray. You count. You do not remember the last time you prayed.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT in darkness - counting on fingers - neighbor silhouette kneeling in prayer through wall - contrast

Content_Sketch: Down to shelter. Plastic bag tears on the stairs. Water bottle rolls under a car. Daughter will not let go of the cat drawing. 12 families in the parking garage. Battery radio. Bread passed between strangers.
Illustration_Note: Stairwell descent - torn bag - families huddled in parking garage - STRANGER giving DAUGHTER his jacket - radio glow

Content_Sketch: A man you never spoke to gives your daughter his jacket. Someone brings bread. Nobody is in charge. Nobody needs to be.
Illustration_Note: Close on STRANGER handing jacket to DAUGHTER - group of stickmen sharing bread in dim light - battery radio between them

Content_Sketch: After the strikes the IRGC sets up checkpoints on Hemmat. Basij patrol the neighborhoods. A man in your building is taken for a satellite dish. He comes back and says nothing. Bombed from above. Controlled from within.
Illustration_Note: Checkpoint scene: BASIJ patrol stickmen with armbands - SUBJECT walking past with head down - military vehicles on Hemmat

Content_Sketch: In the pharmacy. One box of insulin left. The man ahead says his son needs it. You say your daughter needs it. The pharmacist says nothing. You take the box. You look back. He is still standing there.
Illustration_Note: Pharmacy interior - SUBJECT and PHARMACY_MAN facing counter - one box on shelf - SUBJECT taking box - PHARMACY_MAN standing still with empty hands

Content_Sketch: That night your daughter sleeps. You do not.
Illustration_Note: Split: DAUGHTER sleeping with cat drawing in fist - SUBJECT sitting awake in dark - insulin box on table between them

Content_Sketch: Borders close. Price triples then triples again. You calculate: enough for 2 not 3. Wife will not leave without you. You will not leave without mother. Nobody leaves. Everything becomes numbers.
Illustration_Note: Bus station chaos - price board with numbers rising - SUBJECT with WIFE and DAUGHTER - calculator on phone - MOTHER small figure in background

Content_Sketch: Bread queue at 5 AM. Flour gone by 7. Baker comes out says nothing left. Queue dissolves. No one riots. No one checks their phone anymore.
Illustration_Note: Long queue of stickmen at dawn - BAKER in apron at door - queue walking away with empty bags - phones in pockets untouched

Content_Sketch: 30 minutes of internet. Cousin in Canada crying. She says the world is watching. The world watches the explosions. Not the bread queue. Not the man at the pharmacy counter. Daughter asks: whose planes are those? Go draw the cat. She draws the cat inside a box.
Illustration_Note: Split: video call - crying face on screen - cut to DAUGHTER drawing cat inside a box shape - planes in sky through window

Content_Sketch: Ceasefire comes. Nothing returns. Crack running ceiling to floor. Metro closed. Company lost servers. The rial fell further than you can calculate.
Illustration_Note: Apartment interior with crack running down wall - debris - SUBJECT standing looking at damage - grey tones

Content_Sketch: The saffron ice cream shop on Valiasr is closed. Not bombed. The owner left and did not come back. You do not argue about football anymore.
Illustration_Note: Valiasr street - shuttered ice cream shop - empty tree-lined sidewalk - SUBJECT walking past alone - contrast to PHASE_EARLY warmth

Content_Sketch: Daughter flinches at car doors. Wife sleeps with the documents bag touching her arm. You stop counting.
Illustration_Note: Home: DAUGHTER flinching - WIFE asleep with bag at her side - SUBJECT sitting at kitchen table - glass of water untouched

Content_Sketch: You survived. Everyone says that should be enough. It is not.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT standing at window looking out at rebuilt-but-different Tehran skyline - alone - weight posture

Content_Sketch: The cat drawing is still on the fridge. The crack in the wall runs right through it. Your daughter draws the cat again. Inside a box. You almost ask what the box is. You do not ask.
Illustration_Note: Fridge with cat drawing - crack visible through it - DAUGHTER at table drawing new cat inside box - SUBJECT watching from doorway

Content_Sketch: Only one number left. Alive. That is the only number remaining.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT alone - same hoodie as HOOK - phone on table face-down - glass of water still on counter - final hold
